event: admitted to the medical intensive care unit (MICU)
transferred for concern of toxic megacolon
oxygen saturation 97% on 3 l/min nasal cannula
rectal exam positive for gross blood
non-contrast abdomen and pelvis CT scan significant for pancolitis
transferred to surgical intensive care unit (SICU)
normalized leukocytosis and lactic acidosis over next 2 days
non-contrast abdominal CT scan demonstrated pneumoperitoneum
deemed high surgical risk for exploratory laparotomy
taken to operating room for loop ileostomy rescue procedure
repeat endotracheal aspirate culture negative for fungus
non-contrast chest CT scan no evidence of fungal pneumonia
intraoperative small bowel necrosis from distal jejunum to distal ileum
goals of care changed to comfort measures
third endotracheal aspirate culture positive for 2+ A. fumigatus
pathologic evaluation revealed hemorrhagic gangrenous bowel
fungi within bowel wall, artery wall, and lumen
Gomori Methenamine Silver stain characteristic of Aspergillus species
short course corticosteroid use (10 days prednisone)
